content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
Administration 
322·5660 
Mr. Selby Scott Wilson 
Post Office Box 62 
Cantua Creek, CA 93608 
Dear Mr. Wilson: 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
July 15, 1981 
Statements af Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Thank you for your request for written advice regarding 
possible conflicts of interest arising from your election to 
the Cantua Elementary School District Board of Trustees. 
As we understand the material facts, they are as 
follows: 
1. 
Prior to your election to the Board of Trustees, 
your wife was employed as a tenured teacher with the Cantua 
Creek Elementary School District for more than 24 years. 
She 
is still employed in that capacity. 
2. 
Your wife, as a school district employee, has leased 
a horne from the school district. 
These arrangements are 
a part of the benefits which she receives as an employee. 
The rental for the horne is $55/month, which, obviously, is 
less than fair-market-value. 
However, due to the unique 
housing market in rural Cantua Creek, it is not known what 
fair-market-value would bring in rental for the horne. 
But, 
it is safe to assume that the school district is subsidizing 
your rent. 
3. 
The lease between your wife and the school district 
is a month-to-month tenancy, terminable on 30 days written 
notice, and the tenancy requries the tenant ~to comply with 
all rules, regulations and orders of the Governing Board of 
the C&~TUA ELEME~TARY SCHOOL DISTRICT ... ", of which you are 
now a member. 
4. 
Upon your election to the school board, the board 
voted 3-2 to terminate the lease agreement with your wife, 
evicting her and you from the premises. 
This vote was 
based upon advice from the Fresno County Counsel's office 
that you had a conflict of interest under these circumstances 
and that either the lease would have to be terminated or 
you would have to be removed from the board. 


Mr. Selby Scott 
-3-
July IS, 1981 
Therefore, under the Political Reform Act, you need not 
even disqualify yourself in this regard. 
However, as I 
indicated to you over the telephone, there are other provisions 
of lawS/ which may require a different conclusion. 
The 
Commission is not engaged in interpreting those provisions. 
You have indicated that you now have legal counsel and you 
should consult with them in that regard. 
You may also 
wish to contact the Attorney General for an opinion. 
Since 
you have indicated that there may be provisions of the 
Education Code which also apply you may wish to contact 
the State Department of Education as well. 
BL:gs 
Enclosures 
ROBERT E. 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
~For example, Government Code Section 1090, et ~. 


Mr. Selby Scott Wilson 
-2-
July IS, 1981 
You have requested our written advice as to whether 
your situation created any conflict on interest under the 
Political Reform Act.lI 
Initially, it should be remembered that the Political 
Reform Act only requires disqualification as to certain 
decisions, not as to membership. 
Section 87100 prohibits 
a public official from making, participating in making, or 
in any way using his official position to influence the 
making of a decision in which he has a financial interest. 
Thus, if disqualification is required at all, it would only 
be as to those decisions involving your financial interest. 
Financial interest is defined by Section 87103.2/ 
Under 
subdivision (b) you have an indirect interest, through your 
spouse, in the real property you are renting. 
However, that 
interest does not exceed the threshold level of $1,000, since 
the lease is only month-to-month, and there are not restrictions 
on your eviction.l/ 
Under subdivision (c) of Section 87103, a source of 
income of more than $250 during the last 12 months can be 
sufficient to require disqualification. 
However, the source 
of income to your spouse in this case is the school district, 
which is a local government agency.4/ 
Under Section 82030(b), 
"income", as used in the Act, does not include salary and 
reimbursement for expenses received from a local government 
agency. 
The subsidy aspect of this employee housing is a 
form of salary or fringe benefit provided by the school 
district to its employees. 
It is not available to anyone 
else, and terminates upon termination of the employee. 
l/Government Code Sections 81001-91014, (copy enclosed 
for your convenience); all citations are to the Government 
Code unless otherwise specified. 
~/Copy enclosed. 
3/This fact distinguishes your situation from that of 
Zona Sage in our Opinion No. 80-010 Overstreet, 6 FPPC 12 
(March 2, 1981) 
4/Local government agency is defined by Section 82041 
to include school districts.

sections.question: Whether your situation created any conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act arising from your election to the Cantua Elementary School District Board of Trustees, given that your wife is a tenured teacher employed by the district and leases a home from the district at $55/month (below fair market value), and the board voted 3-2 to terminate the lease based on conflict of interest advice from Fresno County Counsel.
sections.conclusion: Therefore, under the Political Reform Act, you need not even disqualify yourself in this regard. However, as I indicated to you over the telephone, there are other provisions of laws which may require a different conclusion. The Commission is not engaged in interpreting those provisions. You have indicated that you now have legal counsel and you should consult with them in that regard. You may also wish to contact the Attorney General for an opinion. Since you have indicated that there may be provisions of the Education Code which also apply you may wish to contact the State Department of Education as well.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a school board member have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when his spouse is a tenured employee of the district receiving subsidized housing as a fringe benefit, and the board voted to terminate that housing arrangement based on conflict of interest concerns?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under the Political Reform Act, the board member does not have a disqualifying financial interest because the spousal housing interest does not exceed the $1,000 threshold and the income subsidy is excluded as a government agency fringe benefit. However, other state laws including Government Code Section 1090 and the Education Code may impose different requirements, and the FPPC recommends consultation with legal counsel, the Attorney General, and the State Department of Education.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document has clear question and conclusion sections. Detailed legal analysis provided with statutory references. Letter references prior telephone conversation and recommends consultation with other authorities on non-FPPC legal issues. Minor formatting inconsistency in letterhead (ampersand before CANTUA appears truncated in original).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether your situation created any conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act arising from your election to the Cantua Elementary School District Board of Trustees, given that your wife is a tenured teacher employed by the district and leases a home from the district at $55/month (below fair market value), and the board voted 3-2 to terminate the lease based on conflict of interest advice from Fresno County Counsel.

CONCLUSION: Therefore, under the Political Reform Act, you need not even disqualify yourself in this regard. However, as I indicated to you over the telephone, there are other provisions of laws which may require a different conclusion. The Commission is not engaged in interpreting those provisions. You have indicated that you now have legal counsel and you should consult with them in that regard. You may also wish to contact the Attorney General for an opinion. Since you have indicated that there may be provisions of the Education Code which also apply you may wish to contact the State Department of Education as well.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter regarding conflict of interest issues for a newly-elected school board member whose spouse is a district employee receiving subsidized housing. The Commission concludes no disqualification is required under the Political Reform Act, though other state laws may apply.
